steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a scholarly prediction (liberalization helps companies raise money) and Henry's empirical finding (11 liberalizing countries saw significant investment increases). The blank must describe what this finding reveals about that prediction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — introduces specific countries or timing: No such detail appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — the standard view failed to anticipate the effects: The opposite — Henry's data matched the prediction. ✗
  • Choice C — unsupported regional claims: The passage does not distinguish regions or timing. ✗
  • Choice D — empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus: Henry found investment increases following liberalization, so the actual outcome matched what scholars predicted. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
